title: Microsoft Announces Major Changes to Xbox Gaming Division
subtitle: The technology giant is cutting thousands of jobs as it reshapes its gaming business strategy

summary.en: Microsoft has announced a significant restructuring of its Xbox gaming division this week. The company plans to lay off over 3,000 employees as part of a broader effort to improve financial health. This move follows earlier reports of larger job cuts affecting the entire global company. In addition to the layoffs, Microsoft is spinning off four game studios to operate as independent businesses. These changes represent a major shift in how the company manages its gaming resources and future projects.
summary.zh: 微軟本週宣布對其 Xbox 遊戲部門進行重大重組。該公司計畫裁減超過三千名員工，作為改善財務狀況的廣泛計畫之一。此舉延續了先前關於全球裁員的報導。除了裁員外，微軟還將分拆四家遊戲工作室，使其轉為獨立企業運作。這些變動代表該公司在管理遊戲資源與未來專案的方式上出現了重大轉變。

paragraphs.en: Microsoft has officially started a major restructuring of its Xbox gaming division this week, marking a difficult period for the company's employees. According to NPR Business, the technology giant has announced plans to lay off more than 3,000 staff members within the gaming department. This decision is part of a larger, global effort by Microsoft to change how it operates and to improve its overall financial health. These job cuts are significant, as they follow previous reports that the company intended to reduce its total workforce by 4,800 people, which represents about 2% of all its employees worldwide.
paragraphs.zh: 微軟（Microsoft）本週正式啟動了 Xbox 遊戲部門的大規模重組，這對該公司的員工而言是一段艱難的時期。根據美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）商業頻道的報導，這家科技巨頭已宣布計畫裁減遊戲部門超過三千名員工。此項決策是微軟全球營運調整計畫的一部分，旨在改善整體財務狀況。此次裁員規模相當顯著，此前已有報導指出該公司計畫在全球裁減四千八百名員工，約佔其全球總員工數的百分之二。

paragraphs.en: The recent announcement is an update to a series of changes that have been happening over the past few days. Earlier reports indicated that the company was looking for ways to make the Xbox division more efficient. As part of this new strategy, Microsoft has decided to spin off four of its game studios. This means these studios will no longer be directly owned or managed by Microsoft and will instead operate as independent companies. By doing this, the company hopes to focus its remaining resources on high-priority projects, such as the popular Fallout series, which the company views as a key part of its future success in the gaming market.
paragraphs.zh: 這項最新公告是過去幾天一系列變動的更新。早先的報導顯示，該公司正尋求提高 Xbox 部門效率的方法。作為新策略的一環，微軟決定將旗下四家遊戲工作室分拆出去。這意味著這些工作室將不再由微軟直接擁有或管理，而是轉為獨立公司運作。透過此舉，微軟希望將剩餘資源集中在優先項目上，例如被視為未來遊戲市場成功關鍵的熱門作品《異塵餘生》（Fallout）系列。

paragraphs.en: Industry experts are watching these developments closely to understand what they mean for the future of the gaming industry. The decision to cut thousands of jobs and change the structure of the business suggests that even large, successful companies are facing pressure to reduce costs and increase profits. For many employees, this is a very challenging time. The news of the layoffs has raised questions about how these changes will affect the quality and number of games that Xbox will offer to players in the coming years. While the company has not provided full details on every project, it is clear that the focus is shifting toward a smaller number of very successful titles.
paragraphs.zh: 產業專家正密切關注這些發展，以了解其對遊戲產業未來的意義。裁減數千名員工並改變業務結構的決定顯示，即使是大型且成功的企業，也面臨著削減成本與提高獲利的壓力。對許多員工來說，這是一個極具挑戰的時刻。裁員消息引發了外界對於這些變動將如何影響 Xbox 未來幾年遊戲品質與數量的疑慮。雖然該公司尚未提供每個項目的完整細節，但顯而易見的是，重心正轉向少數幾款極具潛力的熱門作品。

paragraphs.en: According to reports from NPR Business, the decision to move resources toward specific high-priority projects is a clear sign that Microsoft wants to be more careful with its spending. The gaming industry has grown very quickly in recent years, but it is now facing a period of adjustment. Many companies are finding that they need to be more selective about which games they develop and how many people they employ. By selling or closing certain studios, Microsoft is trying to simplify its business model and ensure that it can continue to compete with other major players in the market.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 NPR 商業頻道的報導，將資源轉向特定優先項目的決策，清楚顯示微軟希望在支出上更加謹慎。遊戲產業近年來成長迅速，但目前正處於調整期。許多公司發現，他們必須更審慎地選擇開發哪些遊戲，並重新評估僱用人數。透過出售或關閉特定工作室，微軟正試圖簡化其商業模式，以確保能持續與市場上的其他主要對手競爭。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the impact of these changes will likely be felt for a long time. The gaming community is waiting to see how the independence of the four studios will change the games they produce. Some fans are worried that these changes might lead to fewer creative choices, while others believe that focusing on fewer, higher-quality games could actually be a positive step for the brand. For now, the priority for Microsoft is to complete this restructuring process as smoothly as possible while managing the difficult task of letting go of thousands of talented workers.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，這些變動的影響可能會持續很長一段時間。遊戲社群正拭目以待，觀察這四家工作室的獨立運作將如何改變其產出的遊戲。部分粉絲擔心這些變動可能導致創意選擇減少，但也有人認為，專注於數量較少但品質更高的遊戲，對該品牌而言可能是一項正面的舉措。目前，微軟的首要任務是在盡可能順利完成重組程序的同時，處理裁減數千名優秀員工這項艱鉅的任務。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, this hard reset of the Xbox division is a reflection of the current economic environment. As companies in the technology sector face rising costs and changing consumer habits, they are often forced to make difficult decisions to stay profitable. Whether this strategy will help Microsoft regain its position as a leader in the gaming world remains to be seen. For the employees affected by these layoffs, the focus is now on finding new opportunities, while the company moves forward with its new, more focused plan for the future of Xbox.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，Xbox 部門的這次重大重整反映了當前的經濟環境。隨著科技業公司面臨成本上升與消費者習慣改變，他們往往被迫做出艱難的決策以維持獲利。這項策略是否能協助微軟重拾遊戲界領導者的地位，仍有待觀察。對於受裁員影響的員工而言，現在的重點是尋找新的機會，而微軟則將繼續推動其針對 Xbox 未來所規劃、更為聚焦的新計畫。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To create a new, independent company from an existing part of a larger company.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 分拆（公司），使之成為獨立運作的企業。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此處指將部門獨立出去，而非字面上的「旋轉」。文中：As part of this new strategy, Microsoft has decided to spin off four of its game studios.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A polite way to say firing or dismissing employees.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意為「放手」，在此語境下為裁員的委婉語。文中：For now, the priority for Microsoft is to complete this restructuring process as smoothly as possible while managing the difficult task of letting go of thousands of talented workers.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A complete restart or a major change to a system to fix problems.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 徹底重啟、大刀闊斧的改革。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原指電子產品的強制重啟，此處比喻公司進行大規模的結構調整。文中：Ultimately, this hard reset of the Xbox division is a reflection of the current economic environment.

sources.name: NPR Business
sources.title: Microsoft is doing a hard reset of its XBOX gaming division

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Announces Major Changes to Xbox Gaming Division
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft is making significant changes to its Xbox gaming business this week. The company announced plans to lay off over 3,000 employees within the division. These moves follow earlier reports of even larger job cuts across the global company. Microsoft aims to focus its resources on its most successful projects. The restructuring also includes selling or closing several smaller game studios.

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Announces Major Job Cuts and Xbox Restructuring
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft has announced that it will cut 4,800 jobs, representing about 2% of its global workforce. A large portion of these layoffs will occur within the Xbox gaming division. The company is spinning off four game studios to operate independently. This decision is part of a larger strategy to focus on artificial intelligence and high-profit gaming projects. Leadership stated that these changes are necessary to keep the company competitive in a fast-changing industry.

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Makes Major Changes to Xbox Business
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft is undergoing a significant restructuring of its Xbox division to improve financial health. The company plans to lay off 3,200 employees and close or sell four studios. Resources are being moved toward high-priority projects like the Fallout series. Industry experts suggest these changes reflect a broader shift in Microsoft's strategy. The future of the Xbox brand remains a subject of intense debate among market analysts.

quiz.question: What is the specific scope of the workforce reduction mentioned in the article?

options.A: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 people from the Xbox division alone.
options.B: The 3,000 gaming staff layoffs are part of a broader plan to cut 4,800 jobs globally.
options.C: Microsoft plans to reduce its total global workforce by 2% starting next year.
options.D: The company is laying off 3,000 employees, which accounts for 2% of its entire global workforce.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第一段，文中指出 Xbox 部門裁員 3,000 人，這是該公司全球裁員 4,800 人（佔總員工數 2%）計畫的一部分。選項 A 錯誤，因為 4,800 人是全球總數而非僅限 Xbox。選項 C 錯誤，因為裁員已經開始，並非明年才開始。選項 D 錯誤，因為 2% 對應的是 4,800 人，而非 3,000 人。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future strategy of the Xbox division?

options.A: Microsoft intends to stop producing games entirely to focus on hardware.
options.B: The company will increase the total number of game titles released annually.
options.C: Microsoft is shifting its focus toward a smaller, more curated portfolio of high-performing games.
options.D: The four independent studios will continue to receive the same level of funding from Microsoft.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段與第三段，Microsoft 希望將資源集中在如 Fallout 等高優先級項目，且文中明確提到「focus is shifting toward a smaller number of very successful titles」。選項 A 與文章內容矛盾。選項 B 與文章提到的「減少數量」相反。選項 D 錯誤，因為這些工作室已轉為獨立營運，不再由 Microsoft 直接管理或擁有。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, the author describes the restructuring as a 'hard reset'. What does this phrase imply in this context?

options.A: A complete technical failure of the gaming software.
options.B: A significant and potentially painful change to the organization's structure.
options.C: A temporary pause in operations to fix minor errors.
options.D: A process of restarting all game consoles in the market.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此文中，「hard reset」並非指電腦重開機，而是比喻公司進行了劇烈且艱難的架構重組，以應對經濟壓力。選項 A 錯誤，這是對「reset」一詞的字面誤解。選項 C 錯誤，因為裁員與分拆工作室是永久性的結構改變，而非暫時性。選項 D 錯誤，這完全脫離了文章討論的商業重組主題。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The gaming industry is currently the most profitable sector in the technology market.
options.B: Microsoft is struggling to compete with other major players due to a lack of talent.
options.C: The four studios being spun off will likely fail without Microsoft's support.
options.D: Microsoft is restructuring its Xbox division to cut costs and focus on core projects amidst economic challenges.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。整篇文章的核心在於描述 Microsoft 如何透過裁員與分拆工作室來因應經濟環境，並將資源集中在關鍵項目以維持競爭力。選項 A 與文章提到的「經濟壓力」不符。選項 B 錯誤，文章並未歸咎於缺乏人才。選項 C 屬於過度推論，文章並未斷言這些工作室會失敗，僅提及粉絲對未來發展的擔憂。
